data.title: Capitol Federal Financial Is Still Cheap Where It Matters Most
data.description:
Way back in September of 2023, one bank that I decided to analyze was Capitol Federal Financial, Inc. (NASDAQ:CFFN). At the time, the firm had experienced significant downside because of the banking crisis that occurred earlier in the year. Despite many of the companies in the industry recovering, it had lagged, with shares still down 36% since February. At the end of the day, I believed that the stock was cheap enough and the company was of a high enough quality to justify a bullish assessment. This led me to rate it a "Buy."

Unfortunately, the picture has not turned out quite as well as I would have hoped for. Even though overall financial performance for the institution continues to improve, low asset quality and a high price to earnings multiple has caused its share price to materially underperform the market. From the time I wrote the article until today, shares are up only 17.8% compared to the 29.6% rise that the S&P 500 (SP500) saw. Even so, I believe that the market continues to misjudge this opportunity. Admittedly, the low asset quality of the business is problematic. And because of that, I do think that having it rated a very soft "Buy" as opposed to a solid "Buy" is appropriate.

Upside remains

Fundamentally speaking, financial performance for Capitol Federal Financial has been pretty good as of late. As an example, we need only take the most recent quarter, which would be the second quarter of the company's 2025 fiscal year. During that time, net interest income for the business was $43.8 million. That translated to a rise compared to the $41 million reported the previous year. This came not only because of a rise in the size of the company's deposit base, and other changes to its balance sheet, but also because the firm benefited from an improvement in its net interest margin from 1.86% to 1.92%. For the first half of 2025 as a whole, net interest income of $85.4 million beat out the $80.5 million reported the previous year, with an improvement in the company's net interest margin from 1.76% to 1.89% helping significantly.

Outside of its core banking activities that bring in net interest income, the firm also has some other operations. Included under non-interest income would be deposit service fees that the business generates. The rise in the second quarter of 2025 from $2.5 million to $2.6 million, as well as the jump in insurance commissions from $0.7 million to $0.9 million, made possible an improvement in the company's non-interest income from $4.6 million to $5 million. And for the first half of the year in its entirety, the absence of the $13.3 million loss that the company saw from securities transactions resulted in its non-interest income going from negative $4.3 million to positive $9.6 million.

These improvements were instrumental in pushing up profitability for the company. For the second quarter of 2025, net income was $15.4 million. That compared favorably to the $13.8 million reported the previous year. Meanwhile, for the first half of the year, net profits jumped from $16.3 million to $30.8 million. Even though there were some one-time items in here such as the aforementioned loss on securities transactions, the overall trend is undeniably positive.

The company's balance sheet is also showing signs of improvement. For the most recent quarter, the value of deposits came in at $6.37 billion. This marks growth compared to the $6.13 billion that the company ended 2024 with. It's also up from the $6.19 billion reported for the end of 2023. One thing that I always look for is exposure to broker deposits. These types of deposits are not inherently bad, but they are more costly and not always reliable. These are essentially deposits that come from other financial institutions. As of the end of the most recent quarter, the firm had no broker deposits, and only 1% of total deposits were public unit certificates of deposit.

Even though there was growth recently when it came to the deposits on the company's books, the value of loans has been a bit lumpy. After skyrocketing from $7.20 billion in 2023 to $7.91 billion in 2024, there was a drop to $7.88 billion In the most recent quarter. Loan composition is certainly an important thing to take into consideration when making an investment decision in a bank. We do know that about 49% of the company's loans are one to four family mortgages that it originated. Another 28.3% of its loan portfolio consists of the same types of assets that were purchased from other parties. And the firm also had a tiny bit of exposure to one to four family mortgages that were dedicated to the construction of such properties.

By comparison, exposure to the commercial space is quite small. If we look at commercial real estate, commercial and industrial loans, and construction loans, we end up with only about 21.1% of the company's overall loan portfolio. And consumer loans account for the remaining 1.4%.

Recently, the value of securities on the company's books have been on the rise. After plummeting from $1.56 billion in 2023 to $856.3 million in 2024, there was an uptick to $961.4 million in the most recent quarter. Cash and cash equivalents have also been on the rise, with an improvement from $217.3 million last year to $340.4 million this year. And over the same window of time, the value of debt has declined from $2.18 billion to $2.14 billion. All things considered, these are some pretty positive moves if you ask me.

When it comes to valuation, the picture for Capitol Federal Financial is undeniably mixed. If we use adjusted net profits from last year that strip out one time events, we still end up with a price to earnings multiple of 17.5. That's well above the 6 to 10 range that I prefer to see in this industry. In the chart above, I compared Capitol Federal Financial to five similar companies. And what I found was that four of the five firms ended up being cheaper than it is. As part of my analysis, I decided to also look at both the price to book and price to tangible book multiples. These can be seen in the chart below. In both of those cases, the amazingly low reading of 0.71 that Capitol Federal Financial scores places that materially lower than any of its competitors that I compared it to. This disparity has played a big role in my decision to remain bullish about the business. The fact that only about 12% of its deposits are uninsured also contributed to this decision.

Besides the high price to earnings multiple, the only thing I really don't like is the fact that asset quality for the institution remains low. In the first chart below, you can see a rather disappointing return on assets reading of only 0.64%. This was lower than all but one of the five companies I compared it to. In the subsequent chart, I did the same type of analysis using return on equity. And once again, four of the five firms ended up being better off than Capitol Federal Financial is.

Takeaway

From the data at my disposal, I would make the case that Capitol Federal Financial, Inc. is far from being a fantastic prospect. But it's certainly not a bad one. The company tilts between being mediocre and being slightly positive, though I have opted to be more optimistic about it. This is based on its low price to book and price to tangible book multiples, the improvements that it has seen so far this year from a revenue and profit perspective, and some of the changes that we have seen with its balance sheet such as a reduction in debt, growing deposits, and growth for both securities and cash. At the end of the day, I believe that maintaining a soft "Buy" rating is the right decision.

data.title: BlackRock Advantage Emerging Markets Fund Q1 2025 Commentary
data.description:

For Investor A shares: Total 1.78%; Net, Including Investment Related Expenses 1.16%. Institutional and Investor A shares have contractual waivers with an end date of 06/30/2026 terminable upon 90 days' notice. For certain share classes, BlackRock may voluntarily agree to waive certain fees and expenses in which the adviser may discontinue at any time without notice. Expenses stated as of the fund's most recent prospectus. Data represents past performance and is no guarantee of future results. Investment returns and principal values may fluctuate so that an investor's shares, when redeemed, may be worth more or less than their original cost. All returns assume reinvestment of dividends and capital gains. Current performance may be lower or higher than that shown. Refer to Investment Management & Financial Services | BlackRock for most recent month-end performance. Investment returns reflect total fund operating expenses, net of all fees, waivers and/or expense reimbursements. Index performance is shown for illustrative purposes only. It is not possible to invest directly in an unmanaged index. Share classes have different fees and other features. Returns with sales charge reflect deduction of current maximum initial sales charge of 5.25% for Investor A shares. Institutional shares have no front- or back-end load. Institutional shares have limited availability and may be purchased at various minimums. See prospectus for details. Net Expenses Excluding Investment Related Expenses for Institutional shares: 0.85%; for Investor A shares:1.15%.

Commentary as of 03/31/25

  • The fund posted returns of 2.91% (Institutional shares) and 2.76% (Investor A shares, without sales charge) for the first quarter of 2025.
  • The fund's slight underperformance of its benchmark was driven by macro-thematic measures and positioning, while stock selection sentiment and fundamental insights provided support.
  • From a sector- and country-positioning perspective, the fund remained largely neutral. The fund had slight overweight allocations to the information technology and communication services sectors, and maintained slight underweight exposures to the utilities and consumer discretionary sectors. It had a slight overweight holding in Taiwan and maintained a slight underweight position in India.

Contributors

Fundamental and sentiment stock selection insights provided support during the quarter. Within fundamental insights, defensive quality measures favoring lower-volatility stocks helped to mitigate some losses as investors sought shelter amid market volatility. Fundamental valuation measures tracking valuations, earnings, and other financial statement data did well amid the market style preference. Insights designed to evaluate both retail and analyst sentiment using machine reading helped to correctly identify emerging market themes and drive a successful underweight exposure to the automobiles industry.

Detractors

The fund's modest underperformance was driven by select country and sector macro-thematic measures. Insights designed to track Indian mutual fund positioning struggled as macroeconomic headwinds continued to overwhelm fundamental drivers of stock prices. Positioning favoring countries that display relative fundamental and macroeconomic attractiveness also suffered, as geopolitics and trade policy took center stage. These motivated incorrect security selection in India and an underweight allocation to the country.

Portfolio management

Jeff Shen, PhD, Gerardo Rodriguez, Richard Mathieson, David Piazza

Top 10 holdings (%)

TSMC (TSM) 9.09
Tencent Holdings (OTCPK:TCEHY) 5.87
Alibaba Group Holding (BABA) 3.65
Samsung Electronics (OTCPK:SSNLF) 2.80
Infosys Ltd (INFY) 1.68
Bharti Airtel Ltd 1.68
MediaTek Inc (OTCPK:MDTTF) 1.65
BYD Co Ltd (OTCPK:BYDDF) 1.65
Ping An Insurance (Group) Co Of China Ltd (OTCPK:PNGAY) 1.49
Meituan (OTCPK:MPNGF) 1.41

Investment approach

Invests at least 80% of its assets in equity securities or other financial instruments that are components of, or have market capitalizations similar to, the securities included in the MSCI Emerging Markets (1) Index.

Further insight

Emerging market equities had a positive quarter, outperforming developed markets amid U.S. tariff policy uncertainty and doubts about valuations linked to artificial intelligence (AI). Latin America led emerging market performance, with Colombia driving gains and Brazil rebounding after a tough 2024. China was the best performer in Asia, led by Al progress and President Xi Jinping's meeting with technology firms. However, the country's performance moderated in March due to tariff uncertainty. Taiwan struggled due to semiconductor industry weakness and tariff concerns. Indian equities rebounded in March, driven by easing regulatory pressure on banks, rupee appreciation, and strong buying by foreign equity investors. In Europe, Poland's positive performance suggested optimism about a potential end to hostilities in Ukraine.

Important Risks: The fund is actively managed and its characteristics will vary. Stock values fluctuate in price so the value of your investment can go down depending on market conditions. International investing involves special risks including, but not limited to political risks, currency fluctuations, illiquidity and volatility. These risks may be heightened for investments in emerging markets. The issuers of unsponsored depositary receipts are not obligated to disclose information that is, in the United States, considered material. The fund may engage in active and frequent trading, resulting in short-term capital gains or losses that could increase an investor's tax liability. Shortselling entails special risks. If the fund makes short sales in securities that increase in value, the fund will lose value. Any loss on short positions may or may not be offset by investing short-sale proceeds in other investments. The fund may use derivatives to hedge its investments or to seek to enhance returns. Derivatives entail risks relating to liquidity, leverage and credit that may reduce returns and increase volatility. Investments in emerging markets may be considered speculative and are more likely to experience hyperinflation and currency devaluations, which adversely affect returns. In addition, many emerging securities markets have lower trading volumes and less liquidity.

The opinions expressed are those of the fund's portfolio management team as of March 31, 2025, and may change as subsequent conditions vary. Information and opinions are derived from proprietary and nonproprietary sources deemed by BlackRock to be reliable, are not necessarily all-inclusive and are not guaranteed as to accuracy.

BlackRock provides compensation in connection with obtaining or using third-party ratings and rankings.

1 MSCI Emerging Markets Index is a free float-adjusted, market capitalization-weighted index that measures equity market performance of emerging markets.

You should consider the investment objectives, risks, charges and expenses of the fund carefully before investing. The prospectus and, if available, the summary prospectus contain this and other information about the fund and are available, along with information on other BlackRock funds, by calling 800-882-0052 or from your financial professional. The prospectus should be read carefully before investing.

data.title: i3 Verticals Focuses On Public Sector, But Catalysts Are Unclear
data.description:
Investment Outlook

i3 Verticals (NASDAQ:IIIV) recently reported its FQ2 2025 financial results, beating earnings per share estimates but missing revenue expectations.

Management expects forward organic revenue growth to be in the single digits as the company has divested from healthcare and focuses its efforts on the public sector.

With little concrete information from the company on capital allocation, including stock buybacks, only moderate topline growth and an uncertain state and local public market spending environment, my initial outlook on IIIV is a neutral Hold.

i3’s Market And Approach

i3 operates in the public sector software market, with offerings aimed at the verticals of transportation, ERP, public safety, K-12 education, enterprise utilities and justice tech.

According to a 2024 market research report by Verified Market Research, the global government software market was an estimated $40 billion in 2023 and is expected to exceed $77 billion by 2031.

If achieved, this growth would represent a reasonably robust CAGR (Compound Annual Growth Rate) of 10.1% from 2024 to 2031.

Factors expected to drive this growth include a growing push for e-government service provision, digital transformation initiatives to improve citizen engagement and government information gathering, regulatory compliance, cloud computing advantages and the need to drive cost efficiencies.

Also, the chart below shows the expected growth trajectory of the public sector software market from 2024 to 2031:

The company operates in government-adjacent markets, which are significant in size.

i3 sells its software and services through its direct sales and marketing efforts as well as through independent software vendors, value-added resellers and other independent sales organizations.

In the most recent quarter, the company's revenue contribution percentage breakdown by type is 70% from software and 26% from payments, as the pie chart shows here:

I’ve prepared a thumbnail SWOT of the company's primary characteristics:

Strengths

  • Vertical submarkets focus, recent divestiture, expanding focus on higher margin offerings

Weaknesses

  • Smaller footprint after strategic sales of business units, seasonal fluctuations

Opportunities

  • Growing government spending in digital systems, integration of AI technologies to enhance offerings, strategic tuck-in acquisitions

Threats

  • Uncertain government spending environment, single sector focus, intense competition from larger players

Recent Financial Trends

Total revenue by quarter (columns) has grown sequentially due to SaaS growth and internal AI solutions creating new sales opportunities. Operating income by quarter (red line) has remained flat in the past two quarters.

Gross profit margin by quarter (blue line) has fluctuated seasonally as normal, with uneven improvement and no discernible trend. Selling and G&A expenses as a percentage of total revenue by quarter (orange line) have also varied seasonally with no obvious trend change. R&D expenses as a function of revenue (purple line) have remained essentially stable in recent quarters despite management’s continued investment in generative AI initiatives.

Earnings per share (Diluted) have generally remained within a range with the exception of a few outlier quarters from segment divestitures and other one-time actions.

(All data in the above charts is GAAP.)

For balance sheet results, IIIV ended the quarter with $7.7 million in cash and equivalents and $12.0 million in total debt, all of which was long term.

In the past 12 months, IIIV’s stock price has grown by 24% vs. that of the benchmark iShares Expanded Technology-Software ETF’s (IGV) gain of 29%, mirroring the benchmark more or less, as the chart indicates below.

Below is a handy major financial and operating metrics table for IIIV:

Metric

Amount

EV/Sales - FWD

3.5

EV/EBITDA - FWD

12.6

Rev. Growth Est. - FWD

-13.6%

FY 2025 EPS Estimate - FWD

$1.05

Price/Sales

Revenue Growth - TTM

5.6%

Net Income Margin

46.5%

EBITDA Margin

14.0%

Market Capitalization

$842,380,000
